title: Custom Starboards
description:
## Summary

Add configurable, plural, per-server "starboards" to Angela Lansbury 9000. Server admins use `/starboard add` to designate a destination channel, a trigger emoji, and a reaction threshold. When any message outside that destination earns the threshold count of that emoji, Angela reposts it (as an embed with attribution, content, media link, jump link, and a live count) into the destination channel. A guild may configure any number of starboards; a single emoji may feed several. Admins can `list`, `edit`, and `remove` boards. Counts stay current as reactions change; deleting the original removes the repost.

Secondary deliverable: the repo's first automated test suite (pytest + pytest-asyncio), with fixtures making the existing DB and Discord objects testable.

## Phases (child issues)

- Phase 0: Test harness foundation
- Phase 1: Persistence + matching
- Phase 2: Event handlers
- Phase 3: Command cog

Phases are sequential: P0 → P1 → P2 → P3.

## Decisions locked

1. Plural starboards per guild (surrogate `id` PK; `guild_id` indexed, non-unique).
2. Emoji fan-out: one emoji may drive multiple boards; no `(guild_id, emoji)` uniqueness.
3. Self-stars and bot reactions both count; never call `reaction.users()`.
4. Cache-first counting via `bot.cached_messages`; `fetch_message` only on cache miss.
5. Post-once then edit; removal edits, never deletes.
6. Link media, do not re-host (broken links after deletion are intentional/privacy).
7. Honour original deletion: delete starboard post(s) + clear entry on `on_raw_message_delete`.
8. In-memory config cache per guild, mirroring `activity_module_settings_helper`; invalidate on mutation.
9. Global rollout (`force_global`); no per-guild gating.
10. Emoji input = string SlashOption + parse (unicode and `<:name:id>`).
11. Eligible sources = all readable channels except each board's own target (ignore-list/NSFW deferred).
12. Tests in-scope; every phase ships with them.

## References

- Plan: `.docs/plans/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.md`
- Plan (visual): `.docs/plans/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.html`
- Research: `.docs/research/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.html`

title: Phase 0: Test harness foundation
description:
## Summary

Stand up the repository's first automated test suite (pytest + pytest-asyncio) before any feature code, so every later phase ships with tests. Make the hardcoded DB singleton testable by sourcing its URL from an environment variable, and provide fixtures for a throwaway DB (with per-test rollback) and for faking Discord objects without a live connection.

## Files

- MODIFY `Pipfile` — add `pytest` and `pytest-asyncio` to `[dev-packages]`.
- MODIFY `db/model/__init__.py` (currently line 3: `DB = SQLAlchemy('sqlite:///bumper-db.sqlite')`) — make the URL env-configurable.
- NEW `tests/__init__.py`
- NEW `tests/conftest.py` — fixtures: temp DB schema, per-test rollback, fake Discord object factories.
- NEW `tests/test_harness_smoke.py` — proves the harness runs.
- NEW `pytest.ini` (or `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` in a config file) — set `asyncio_mode = auto`.

## Implementation Details

The DB is a hardcoded singleton at `db/model/__init__.py:3`. Tests must not touch the real `bumper-db.sqlite`. Make the URL env-driven while preserving prod behaviour:

```python
# db/model/__init__.py
import os
from sqla_wrapper import SQLAlchemy

DB = SQLAlchemy(os.environ.get('DATABASE_URL', 'sqlite:///bumper-db.sqlite'))
```

`tests/conftest.py` must set `DATABASE_URL` to a temp sqlite file BEFORE importing `db` (pytest imports conftest before collecting test modules, so module-level env setup here runs first). `sqla_wrapper` provides the needed primitives (verified against the installed lib): `DB.create_all()`, `DB.drop_all()`, and `DB.test_transaction(savepoint=True)` (rolls back everything at the end).

```python
# tests/conftest.py
import os, tempfile, pytest
os.environ.setdefault('DATABASE_URL', f'sqlite:///{tempfile.mkstemp(suffix=".sqlite")[1]}')

import db                       # noqa: imports DB + every model via db/__init__.py
from db import DB

@pytest.fixture(scope='session', autouse=True)
def _schema():
    DB.create_all()
    yield
    DB.drop_all()

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def db_tx():
    tx = DB.test_transaction(savepoint=True)
    yield
    tx.close()
```

Add minimal Discord fake factories in conftest (plain objects or `unittest.mock.MagicMock`), exposing only the attributes the production code reads:

- `make_payload(emoji=..., channel_id=..., message_id=..., guild_id=..., member=...)` — shaped like `nextcord.RawReactionActionEvent` (attrs: `guild_id`, `channel_id`, `message_id`, `user_id`, `emoji`, `member`, `event_type`).
- `make_emoji(name, id=None, animated=False)` — shaped like `nextcord.PartialEmoji`; `str()` must render the unicode char when `id is None`, else `<:name:id>` / `<a:name:id>`.
- `make_message(reactions=[...], content=..., attachments=[...], author=...)` — `reactions` are objects with `.emoji` and `.count`.

These factories are reused by P1/P2/P3 tests.

## Gotchas & Context

- **Import order is the critical risk.** If any module imports `db` before conftest sets `DATABASE_URL`, the engine binds to the prod file. Keep the `os.environ.setdefault('DATABASE_URL', ...)` line at the very top of `tests/conftest.py`, above `import db`.
- `db/__init__.py` already imports every model module; the session-scoped `DB.create_all()` will build the whole schema (including future starboard tables once P1 registers them).
- The project uses `pipenv`. Dev deps are installed with `pipenv install --dev`.
- nextcord `PartialEmoji`: `is_custom_emoji()` is `id is not None`; `__str__` renders unicode vs `<:name:id>` (nextcord/partial_emoji.py:160-192).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `pipenv install --dev` installs pytest + pytest-asyncio; they appear in `Pipfile`/`Pipfile.lock`.
- [ ] `db/model/__init__.py` reads `DATABASE_URL` with the prod sqlite path as default (prod behaviour unchanged when the env var is unset).
- [ ] `pipenv run pytest` runs green.
- [ ] `tests/test_harness_smoke.py` asserts: (a) the active DB URL is NOT the prod `bumper-db.sqlite`, (b) a trivial model insert + query works inside the rollback transaction, (c) an `async def` test executes (proving `asyncio_mode = auto`).
- [ ] No test run writes to the real `bumper-db.sqlite`.

## References

- Plan: `.docs/plans/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.md` (Phase 0)
- Research: `.docs/research/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.html`
- Epic: angela-lansbury-9000-17p

title: Phase 1: Persistence + matching
description:
## Summary

Add the two database tables (`starboard_config`, `starboard_entry`), an Alembic migration, and the cached CRUD helper plus the canonical `emoji_matches` function reused by the handler and the cog. This is the persistence + matching foundation for all later phases.

## Files

- NEW `db/model/starboard_config.py`
- NEW `db/model/starboard_entry.py`
- MODIFY `db/__init__.py` — add `from db.model.starboard_config import *` and `from db.model.starboard_entry import *` (it explicitly lists every model so Alembic + create_all see them; see lines 8-19). Re-export `StarboardConfig`/`StarboardEntry` so `from db import StarboardConfig, StarboardEntry` works, mirroring how `AutoDeleteChannelConfig` is exposed.
- NEW `db/migrations/<date>_<hash>_add_starboard_tables.py` (autogenerated)
- NEW `db/helpers/starboard_helper.py` — CRUD + per-guild cache + `emoji_matches`.
- NEW `tests/test_starboard_helper.py`

## Implementation Details

### Models (SQLAlchemy 2.0 `Mapped`/`mapped_column`, matching existing models)

```python
# db/model/starboard_config.py
class StarboardConfig(DB.Model):
    __tablename__ = 'starboard_config'
    id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(primary_key=True)
    guild_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(index=True)
    target_channel_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column()
    emoji: Mapped[str] = mapped_column()                  # unicode char OR custom name
    emoji_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(nullable=True)  # set only for custom emoji
    threshold: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(default=5)
    enabled: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(default=True)
    name: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(nullable=True)

# db/model/starboard_entry.py
class StarboardEntry(DB.Model):
    __tablename__ = 'starboard_entry'
    id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(primary_key=True)
    starboard_config_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(ForeignKey('starboard_config.id'), index=True)
    guild_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(index=True)
    original_message_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(index=True)
    original_channel_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column()
    posted_message_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(nullable=True)
    author_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column()
    star_count: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(default=0)
    __table_args__ = (UniqueConstraint('starboard_config_id', 'original_message_id'),)
```

Unique constraint on `(starboard_config_id, original_message_id)` makes each board post a given message at most once and makes concurrent inserts collide deterministically. NO uniqueness on `(guild_id, emoji)` — fan-out is intentional.

### Helper (`db/helpers/starboard_helper.py`)

Follow the in-memory cache pattern in `db/helpers/activity_module_settings_helper.py` (module-level dict keyed by `guild_id`). Use the `DB.s` API (`DB.s.first`, `DB.s.all`, `DB.s.query`, `DB.s.add`, `DB.s.delete`, `DB.s.commit`). API surface:

- `get_enabled_configs(guild_id) -> list[StarboardConfig]` — cached; hot path for the reaction handler.
- `get_configs(guild_id)`, `get_config(config_id)`, `add_config(...)`, `update_config(config_id, **kw)`, `remove_config(config_id)` — every mutator INVALIDATES the guild's cache entry.
- `get_entry(config_id, message_id)`, `upsert_entry(...)`, `delete_entries_for_message(guild_id, message_id) -> list[StarboardEntry]` (returns removed rows so the delete handler in P2 knows which posts to remove).
- `emoji_matches(payload_emoji, config) -> bool` — CANONICAL matcher: when `config.emoji_id` is set, match `payload_emoji.id == config.emoji_id`; else match `str(payload_emoji) == config.emoji`. (Unicode emoji have `id is None`; custom should be matched by id since names can be reused.)

### Migration

Generate with `pipenv run python cli.py db revision "add starboard tables"` (proper-cli autogenerate compares models to DB). Verify `upgrade()` creates both tables, the indexes, and the unique constraint; then `pipenv run python cli.py db upgrade`. Migration files follow `YYYY_MM_DD_<hash>_<desc>.py` (template: db/migrations/2024_03_19_c7a45ca9ec78_added_poll_models.py).

## Gotchas & Context

- **Model registration:** if `db/__init__.py` does not import the new model modules, `create_all`/autogenerate silently skip the tables AND the P0 test schema won't include them. This is the most common failure here.
- `DB.s.first(Model, **attrs)` returns one row or None; `DB.s.all(Model, **attrs)` returns a list — used throughout existing helpers.
- Duplicate-insert handling: catch `sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError` + `DB.s.rollback()` (pattern in db/helpers/birthday_helper.py:14-21).
- The cache pattern to copy: `db/helpers/activity_module_settings_helper.py` (module-level `__CACHE` dict; populate on read, drop the guild key on mutation).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Both models exist, are imported in `db/__init__.py`, and re-exported from the `db` package.
- [ ] A new Alembic migration creates both tables + indexes + the unique constraint; `pipenv run python cli.py db upgrade` applies cleanly.
- [ ] Tests: insert/read a config; multiple configs per guild; multiple configs sharing one emoji (fan-out) all persist.
- [ ] Tests: `emoji_matches` — unicode match and non-match; custom match by id even when names differ; custom-vs-unicode mismatch.
- [ ] Tests: `get_enabled_configs` returns only enabled rows; a mutator invalidates the cache so the next read reflects the change.
- [ ] Tests: `upsert_entry` is idempotent on `(config, message)`; a duplicate insert raises `IntegrityError` and the rollback path is exercised; `delete_entries_for_message` returns all matching rows across boards.
- [ ] `pipenv run pytest` green; no test touches the real DB.

## References

- Plan: `.docs/plans/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.md` (Phase 1)
- Research: `.docs/research/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.html`
- Epic: angela-lansbury-9000-17p; depends on Phase 0 (angela-lansbury-9000-17p.1)

title: Phase 2: Event handlers
description:
## Summary

Implement all starboard gateway-event handling: reaction add/remove (cache-first counting, fan-out, post-once-then-edit) and original-message-delete cleanup, plus the pure `reaction_count` helper and the `starboard_embed` builder these need. Extend the existing reaction event closures rather than registering new ones.

## Files

- NEW `bot/cogs/starboard/__init__.py`
- NEW `bot/cogs/starboard/starboard_utils.py` — `reaction_count(message, config)` (pure).
- MODIFY `bot/utils/messages.py` — add `starboard_embed(...)`.
- NEW `bot/events/handlers/starboard_handler.py` — `handle_reaction_add`, `handle_reaction_remove`, `handle_message_delete`, `post_or_edit`.
- MODIFY `bot/events/on_raw_reaction_add_event.py` — call the starboard handler alongside `temp_discussion_handler` (do NOT register a second `on_raw_reaction_add`).
- MODIFY `bot/events/on_raw_reaction_remove_event.py` — same.
- NEW `bot/events/on_raw_message_delete_event.py`
- MODIFY `bot/app.py` — register the message-delete event in `run()` (alongside the existing reaction event registrations at lines 72-73).
- NEW `tests/test_starboard_handler.py`, `tests/test_starboard_utils.py`

## Implementation Details

### Pure helpers (tested in isolation, no mocks)

- `reaction_count(message, config) -> int`: find the matching `Reaction` in `message.reactions` (reuse `starboard_helper.emoji_matches`) and return `.count`, else `0`.
- `messages.starboard_embed(message, emoji_display, count, source_channel) -> nextcord.Embed`: `set_author(name=message.author.display_name, icon_url=message.author.display_avatar.url)`; `description = message.content`; if the message has an image attachment, `set_image(url=<first image attachment .url>)` (reuse `message_has_image`, bot/utils/messages.py:11-16); add a footer/field `{emoji_display} {count} · #{source_channel}`; `timestamp = message.created_at`; include `message.jump_url`. An embed inlines only one image; list extra/non-image attachments as links.

### Reaction handler

Extend the existing closure (keeps `temp_discussion_handler` working):

```python
# bot/events/on_raw_reaction_add_event.py
def register_event(bot):
    @bot.event
    async def on_raw_reaction_add(payload):
        await temp_discussion_handler.handle_closure_react(bot, payload)
        await starboard_handler.handle_reaction_add(bot, payload)
```

- `handle_reaction_add(bot, payload)`: return if `payload.guild_id is None`; `configs = starboard_helper.get_enabled_configs(payload.guild_id)`; `matches = [c for c in configs if starboard_helper.emoji_matches(payload.emoji, c) and payload.channel_id != c.target_channel_id]`; return early if none (the common case — no I/O); resolve the message CACHE-FIRST via `nextcord.utils.get(bot.cached_messages, id=payload.message_id)`, falling back to `bot_utils.get_or_fetch_channel(bot, payload.channel_id)` + `channel.fetch_message(...)` (handle None / `nextcord.NotFound`); for each match compute `reaction_count(message, c)` and call `post_or_edit`.
- `post_or_edit(bot, config, message, count, source_channel)`: `entry = starboard_helper.get_entry(config.id, message.id)`. If `entry is None` and `count >= config.threshold`: build the embed, `send` to the target channel (`get_or_fetch_channel`), then `upsert_entry` with `posted_message_id` + `star_count`. If `entry` exists: fetch `entry.posted_message_id` in the target channel and `edit` its embed to refresh the count (and update `star_count`). Wrap sends/edits in `try/except (nextcord.Forbidden, nextcord.NotFound)` → Sentry, matching the auto-delete error handling (bot/cogs/auto_delete/auto_delete_commands.py:53-71).
- `handle_reaction_remove(bot, payload)`: same matching; if an entry exists, recompute count and edit the post. NEVER delete on un-react.

### Delete propagation

`bot/events/on_raw_message_delete_event.py` registers `on_raw_message_delete(payload)` → `starboard_handler.handle_message_delete(bot, payload)`. The handler: `entries = starboard_helper.delete_entries_for_message(payload.guild_id, payload.message_id)`; for each entry resolve the target channel and delete `posted_message_id` (tolerate `NotFound`/`Forbidden`). `nextcord.RawMessageDeleteEvent` carries `message_id`, `channel_id`, `guild_id` and fires for uncached messages too. No new intents needed (`messages` + `guilds` already enabled in bot/app.py:18).

## Gotchas & Context

- **Handler chaining is the #1 gotcha:** `@bot.event` keeps only ONE callback per event name. Adding a second `@bot.event async def on_raw_reaction_add` would silently clobber `temp_discussion_handler`. The starboard call MUST be added inside the existing closures. Wrap the starboard call (try/except → Sentry) so an exception there doesn't swallow the closure handler and vice versa.
- **No count in the payload:** `RawReactionActionEvent` has no reaction count — that's why we read it from the (cached) `message.reactions`.
- **Cache-first matters:** nextcord keeps `Reaction.count` live in memory for cached messages (max_messages default 1000), so cache hits cost zero REST calls. Only `fetch_message` on a cache miss. A 404 on a deleted message is safe (does not count toward Discord's invalid-request ban).
- `payload.member` is populated on ADD events only; remove events have no member.
- Fetch helpers exist: `bot/utils/bot_utils.py` (`get_or_fetch_guild`, `get_or_fetch_channel`).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pure-helper tests: `reaction_count` returns the right count, `0` when the emoji is absent, and the correct value among multiple reactions; `starboard_embed` includes author/description/jump-url/count, sets an image only when an image attachment exists, and handles empty content (media-only message).
- [ ] Handler tests (fake bot/message/payload, no network): below threshold → no entry, no send; crossing threshold → exactly one send and an entry with `posted_message_id`; an already-posted message getting another reaction → edit path, no second send; a reaction inside the board's own target channel → skipped; fan-out → a message matching two boards posts to both targets with two entries; cache hit does NOT call `fetch_message` (assert the mock is uncalled) while a cache miss does; self-star / bot reaction still counts; remove below threshold edits (not deletes) the post.
- [ ] Delete tests: deleting an original deletes the posted message(s) and clears entries across all boards it fed; deleting a message with no entry is a no-op; a `NotFound` while deleting the post is swallowed and the entry is still cleared.
- [ ] `bot/app.py` `run()` registers the new message-delete event; both reaction events call the starboard handler in addition to `temp_discussion_handler`.
- [ ] `pipenv run pytest` green; no test touches the real DB.

## References

- Plan: `.docs/plans/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.md` (Phase 2)
- Research: `.docs/research/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.html`
- Epic: angela-lansbury-9000-17p; depends on Phase 1 (angela-lansbury-9000-17p.2)

title: Phase 3: Command cog
description:
## Summary

Add the admin-facing `/starboard` command cog (`add|list|edit|remove`), the pure `parse_emoji_input` helper it relies on, the cog/event registrations, and the README "Running tests" section. After this phase the feature is fully wired and shippable.

## Files

- MODIFY `bot/cogs/starboard/starboard_utils.py` — add `parse_emoji_input(raw)`.
- NEW `bot/cogs/starboard/starboard_commands.py` — `StarboardCommands(commands.Cog)`.
- MODIFY `bot/app.py` — `bot.add_cog(StarboardCommands(bot))` in `run()` (alongside the other `add_cog` calls, lines 61-68).
- MODIFY `bot/cogs/__init__.py` — export `StarboardCommands` if following the existing pattern.
- MODIFY `README.md` — add a "Running tests" section (`pipenv install --dev`, `pipenv run pytest`, and the `DATABASE_URL` override note).
- MODIFY `Pipfile.lock` — regenerated by `pipenv install --dev`.
- NEW `tests/test_starboard_commands.py`

## Implementation Details

### Emoji parser (pure)

`parse_emoji_input(raw: str) -> tuple[str, int | None]`: regex `<a?:(\w+):(\d+)>` → `(name, int(id))` for custom emoji; otherwise treat as unicode → `(raw.strip(), None)`. Raise `ValueError` on empty/garbage input. Lives alongside `reaction_count` in `bot/cogs/starboard/starboard_utils.py`.

### Command cog

Root group gated with `default_member_permissions=Permissions(manage_guild=True)` and `force_global=True` (mirrors bot/cogs/server_admin/server_admin_commands.py:10-15). Subcommands:

- `add(channel: nextcord.TextChannel, emoji: str, threshold: int, name: str = optional)`: `parse_emoji_input(emoji)`; if a custom id was parsed, validate it belongs to the guild via `nextcord.utils.get(interaction.guild.emojis, id=...)` and reject foreign emoji; preflight `channel.permissions_for(interaction.guild.me)` for `send_messages` + `embed_links` (pattern: auto_delete_commands.py:144-147) and warn if missing; `starboard_helper.add_config(...)` (which invalidates the cache); confirm with `messages.success(...)`.
- `list()`: build an embed of the guild's boards (target · emoji · threshold · enabled · name); paginate only if needed (pattern options: bot/cogs/server_admin/check_perms_view.py ListPageSource or the hand-rolled view in image_message_delete).
- `edit(starboard: <autocomplete>, threshold?, enabled?, channel?, emoji?)`: update only provided fields; invalidate cache.
- `remove(starboard: <autocomplete, value=id>)`: `starboard_helper.remove_config(id)` (deletes config + its entries); invalidate cache.

Use typed `SlashOption`s for channel/threshold. For emoji, take a string then `parse_emoji_input`; on `ValueError` reply `messages.error(...)` ephemerally. Autocomplete for `remove`/`edit` via `@cmd.on_autocomplete('starboard')` → `interaction.response.send_autocomplete({label: str(id)})` (pattern: bot/cogs/polls/poll_commands.py:52-60); label = `name` or `#channel · emoji ·≥N`.

Confirm `run()` registers the cog and all event modules (the two reaction events already wired in P2 + the new message-delete event).

## Gotchas & Context

- No existing command takes an emoji input — `parse_emoji_input` is new ground; cover unicode, custom, animated custom, and garbage.
- `default_member_permissions=Permissions(manage_guild=True)` is the project's admin-gating mechanism; `force_global=True` ships globally (no `guild_ids` gate).
- Cache invalidation on every mutation (`add`/`edit`/`remove`) is required so the P2 hot path sees changes.
- Full interaction round-trips are hard to unit-test; extract and test the underlying logic (emoji validation against guild, the label/display builder, and the helper CRUD paths).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `/starboard add|list|edit|remove` exist, are restricted to `manage_guild`, and are registered globally.
- [ ] `remove`/`edit` offer autocomplete over the guild's boards.
- [ ] Custom and unicode emoji both work; foreign/garbage emoji are rejected with a clear ephemeral error.
- [ ] Each mutation invalidates the per-guild config cache.
- [ ] Tests: `parse_emoji_input` (unicode 📖 → `('📖', None)`; `<:book:123>` → `('book', 123)`; animated `<a:book:123>`; empty/garbage → `ValueError`); emoji-validation-against-guild; label/display builder; create/update/remove paths through `starboard_helper` assert DB state + cache invalidation.
- [ ] README documents `pipenv install --dev`, `pipenv run pytest`, and the `DATABASE_URL` override.
- [ ] Manual smoke test in the test guild passes: add a board, react past threshold in another channel, confirm the repost, add a reaction (count edits), remove reactions (post stays), delete the original (post disappears).
- [ ] Whole suite green: `pipenv run pytest`; no test touches the real `bumper-db.sqlite`.

## References

- Plan: `.docs/plans/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.md` (Phase 3)
- Research: `.docs/research/2026-06-22-custom-starboards.html`
- Epic: angela-lansbury-9000-17p; depends on Phase 1 (angela-lansbury-9000-17p.2) and Phase 2 (angela-lansbury-9000-17p.3)
